ending: Rezkin wins both the melee and fifth-tier tournament as Dark Tidings. When seven strikers attempt to arrest him, two wait to examine King Bordran's certificate and ultimately swear fealty, while the other five die fighting him. Caydean's forces turn the arena lockdown into a wider campaign against foreigners, so Rezkin organizes fighters, companions, and spectators into an escape column. They breach the Duke of Scutton's estate, evacuate surviving captives from its slave prison, and flee through the sea cave. The young swordwoman executes the duke for his crimes and her father's apparent murder. Frisha’s younger cousin is killed while shielding the young swordwoman during the final fight. A former royal-navy commander carries the survivors away on his merchant ship. At sea, Rezkin unmasks before the traveling party and identifies himself as Dark Tidings and the true king. Frisha, Tam, Reaylin, Wesson, Kai, Tieran, Jimson, Drascon, Millens, the aligned strikers, and the other refugees are fugitives pursued by the royal navy. Caydean has targeted their houses and General Marcum has fled. Rezkin's exact royal lineage, Bordran's complete plan, Striker Farson's location, and the fortress order remain unresolved.
in_short: Rezkin leads Frisha and their companions toward the king's tournament while cultivating noble allies and investigating the authority left to him by King Bordran. Kai, a former striker, identifies Rezkin as the rightful king, and Wesson verifies both his royal certificate and his unusual magic. In Scutton, Rezkin competes secretly as Dark Tidings, builds public support, survives surveillance and poison plots, and discovers the host duke's hidden slave prison. King Caydean's attempt to arrest Dark Tidings expands into mass detention of foreigners. Rezkin wins the tournament, kills five attacking strikers, and leads an armed evacuation through the duke's estate. The duke is executed, surviving captives are rescued, and Frisha's younger cousin dies protecting the young swordwoman. The refugees escape by sea, where Rezkin reveals that he is both Dark Tidings and the true king. Caydean moves against their families and sends the royal navy after them as they seek foreign refuge.

recaps.text: Previously: Rezkin emerged from a hidden upbringing with extraordinary martial and practical training, two royal blades, and authority connected to the late King Bordran. He formed close ties with Frisha, her sworn escort Tamarin Blackwater, the warrior Reaylin de Voss, and Captain Jimson. Rezkin sought General Marcum's approval to marry Frisha, but Marcum rejected the proposal despite respecting Rezkin professionally. Rezkin also operated a covert network as the Raven, while keeping his identities and purposes divided among those around him. King Caydean ruled Ashai after Bordran's death, Prince Thresson was missing, and serious questions remained about the royal succession. Rezkin's former trainer Striker Farson was still at large and represented the best available lead to the hidden fortress, its final orders, and the reason Rezkin had been prepared there. As the new journey begins, Rezkin is committed to protecting his friends, understanding the authority he carries, and taking the group to the king's tournament.

Rezkin takes command of the tournament voyage by assuming the manner of a powerful high noble. He uses the travelers' deference to defend Frisha and Tamarin from class-based insults and to encourage Frisha's relatives and Tieran Nirius to treat them with respect. When a falling beam injures Rezkin, Reaylin's natural healing power awakens. She resists the discovery, but later heals a donkey and a gravely injured prisoner under the instruction of Wesson, a reluctant destructive mage whom Rezkin hires as her teacher. The prisoner, Kai, has admitted killing the mayor's son, but Rezkin stops a corrupt and hurried trial so the charge can be investigated. Kai recognizes signs that Rezkin possesses royal sword-bearer authority and demands proof. Under a mage oath, Wesson authenticates King Bordran's certificate and two Shailen blades, confirms that they are keyed to Rezkin, and finds that Bordran freed them from the reigning king's bond. Kai swears fealty to Rezkin as king. Rezkin accepts responsibility for protecting him but rejects the title and orders secrecy. Kai then accuses Caydean of killing Bordran and abusing Prince Thresson, though neither claim has been independently established.

Rezkin uncovers evidence that the mayor and dismissed magistrate covered up the rape committed by the mayor's son. He kills the two officials while making their deaths appear to result from a mutual struggle. The baron later dismisses the charge against Kai, who joins the voyage. During shipboard practice, Rezkin defeats Kai and exposes his mastery of two blades. Kai identifies Rezkin's dead fortress trainers as strikers and learns that Rezkin killed fourteen of them during his final test while Farson escaped. Rezkin does not know who issued the final order or whom it truly targeted. Tamarin recoils from Rezkin's history, but Frisha argues that killings carried out as lawful duties require context. After reflection, Tam apologizes and swears fealty to Rezkin. Economic decline, taxation, and military musters under Caydean are worsening across Ashai. Kai tells the young nobles that Bordran named an alternative successor, while concealing that it is Rezkin. Wesson notices a possible magical effect that discourages scrutiny of Rezkin's assumed roles. At Port Manai, Hilith Gadderand approaches Rezkin believing the Raven ordered her to spy on him. Wesson again authenticates the claimant's authority, and Reaylin reveals her rebel allegiance before swearing to the unnamed true king through Rezkin.

Rezkin accepts Reaylin's oath on behalf of the unnamed true king but refuses similar offers from Frisha and the young nobles because association with the claimant could endanger their houses. Wesson identifies the subtle spell on Rezkin: a plausible disguise or assumed role becomes unusually convincing, and observers are discouraged from questioning it. As the Raven, Rezkin takes command of the Razor Edges and then establishes control in Scutton by destroying the defiant Ghouls guild and threatening the Stalkers. Meanwhile, Reaylin begins functioning as the party's healer, Wesson explores constructive applications for his destructive power, and Tieran becomes increasingly receptive to a lawful alternative to Caydean. The party reaches Scutton for the king's tournament, where Tieran stays with the Duke of Scutton. Rezkin plans to enter without letting the strikers recognize him. He and his closest allies choose the name Dark Tidings, and a swordsmith supplies a distinctive black blade. Wesson creates a mask and enchantments that hide the wearer's features and prevent observers from remembering the disguised voice. With black clothing, altered hair, the new sword, and the secured mask, Dark Tidings is ready for the tournament while most of Rezkin's companions believe he will not compete.

Dark Tidings registers for the melee and fifth-tier duels, passes the trial by disarming a royal-guard captain, and sponsors qualified commoners who cannot afford entry. His skill attracts public support and close attention from Caydean's strikers, though he repeatedly evades their surveillance. At the Duke of Scutton's estate, another noble guest directs mage power at Rezkin. Rezkin then finds triania poison on Tieran's intended practice equipment and moves him to safety. After seeing Bordran's certificate and learning about Rezkin's concealment spell, Tieran realizes that Rezkin is the named heir. Wesson's blood test establishes that the two men are close relatives without determining the exact relationship. Tieran swears fealty, and Rezkin tells him that he is Dark Tidings. Poison later strikes Frisha's house heir before a tournament bout, but Rezkin administers an antidote and the count recovers. Rumors of a true king spread while Rezkin covertly pressures the host duke to support the rising claimant. Investigating heavily warded passages beneath the duke's manor, Rezkin, Wesson, and Tieran discover a hidden slave prison. Dozens have been confined and starved, only a fraction appear likely to survive, and records expose the duke's trade and a concealed sea route.

Rezkin covertly supplies the surviving captives while arranging a ship and preparing to leave after the tournament. A common-born fifth-tier swordwoman learns that Rezkin is both the true king and Dark Tidings, swears fealty, and accepts his guardianship. A former fortress striker then recognizes Rezkin and claims he was created to serve an unknown master. Rezkin shows him Bordran's certificate and offers the strikers a lawful basis for changing allegiance, but the incomplete fortress plan remains unexplained. Dark Tidings wins the tournament. Seven strikers try to arrest him for treason; two pause to inspect his proof and swear fealty, while he kills the other five. Caydean's forces seal the arena and begin detaining foreigners for ransom, enslavement, or death. Rezkin forms an armed escape column, fights through Scutton, and breaches the Duke of Scutton's estate. The young swordwoman executes the duke, and the group evacuates surviving captives through the sea cave. Frisha's younger cousin dies protecting the swordwoman during the final fight. The refugees board a merchant ship commanded by a former royal-navy officer. At sea, Rezkin removes the Dark Tidings mask and confirms to the travelers that he is the true king. Caydean has targeted their families and replaced General Marcum, who fled rather than obey. With the royal navy pursuing, Rezkin's coalition sails toward foreign refuge.
